name: Olive Garden Tomato/Basil Crostini
recipeIngredient: 2 6-inch Boboli bread shells or similar Italian flatbread
2 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil (10 ml)
1 1/2 cups Roma tomatoes, seeded and diced
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F.
2. For the garlic-oil mixture, soak 1 clove of minced garlic in 2 tablespoons of olive oil for at least 1 hour before using.
3. Line a sheet pan or cookie sheet with foil.
4. Drizzle the garlic-oil over the Boboli bread shells and sprinkle with parmesan cheese.
5. Bake for 4 to 5 minutes, until hot but not crisped or dried.
6. Cut the baked bread into wedges or squares (6 wedges or 2x2-inch squares).
7. Remove from oven and place on a serving plate. Cover generously with cold tomato/basil topping.
8. Serve immediately.
TOPPING: Blend all ingredients for the tomato/basil topping thoroughly and refrigerate for 2 hours before serving. Just prior to serving, drain excess liquid in a colander or strainer. (My Note: I prefer minced garlic mixed with olive oil). Source: The Olive Garden. Recipe by:
